description:
Sub-task 3 follow-up to livespec li-qt7t5t. The upstream spec (livespec PR #184 v075) and the impl-plaintext sub-spec (PR #18 v003) declare that capture-impl-gaps's detection step invokes the sibling detect-impl-gaps thin-transport skill. Currently the capture-impl-gaps SKILL.md prose (Step 1) still describes scanning the spec via the Spec Reader directly, which duplicates detect-impl-gaps's logic in-skill and contradicts the contract ("no in-skill duplication of the detection logic; both this skill and doctor consume the same canonical surface").

This is a pure SKILL.md prose change. No Python edits required. Step 1 should be rewritten to invoke `detect-impl-gaps --json` via subprocess for the authoritative gap-id set, and (optionally) without `--json` for rich human-readable context to display alongside per-gap user consent. The opening prose and frontmatter description should be updated to reflect that `detect-impl-gaps` (not the Spec Reader directly) is the load-bearing surface this skill composes.

Acceptance:
- Step 1 of skills/capture-impl-gaps/SKILL.md describes subprocess invocation of detect-impl-gaps.
- The frontmatter description and opening prose no longer reference scanning via the Spec Reader directly.
- No Python changes (the existing Python primitives are unchanged).
- The lefthook + just check aggregates pass.
title: Refactor capture-impl-gaps SKILL.md Step 1 to invoke detect-impl-gaps --json (Sub-task 3 of livespec li-qt7t5t)

description: Close-out: capture-impl-gaps SKILL.md Step 1 refactored to invoke detect-impl-gaps.
reason: Closed. The capture-impl-gaps SKILL.md is rewritten to invoke the sibling detect-impl-gaps thin-transport skill instead of scanning the spec via Spec Reader directly. Changes: (1) frontmatter description updated to drop the Spec Reader phrasing; (2) opening prose updated so detect-impl-gaps + store are named as the load-bearing surfaces; (3) Step 1 rewritten to subprocess detect-impl-gaps --json for the authoritative gap-id set, plus a second invocation without --json for the rich line form, joined by gap_id for human display; (4) Step 3 updated to use the gap_id from Step 1 (was: derive locally) and correct the gap_id format documentation; (5) §What this skill does NOT do refined to note all spec reads are delegated. Pure SKILL.md prose change; no Python edits. Full just check aggregate (158 tests, 100% coverage) passes.
title: Refactor capture-impl-gaps SKILL.md Step 1 to invoke detect-impl-gaps --json (Sub-task 3 of livespec li-qt7t5t)

description:
Cross-repo follow-up from livespec's /livespec:revise v081 (PC #2: coordinating-epic-stale-revise-enforcement, cross-cutting epic). This is the child PC `work-item-merge-evidence` cited in the parent PC's §"Coordinating-epic header".

Scope (per the child PC at livespec-impl-plaintext/SPECIFICATION/proposed_changes/work-item-merge-evidence.md if it has been filed; otherwise FILE THAT PC FIRST via /livespec:propose-change against this repo's spec target):
- Extend the work-items JSONL schema with a `merge_evidence` field   that records the merged-to-master commit + branch + PR for every   closed work-item that traversed a feature branch.
- Implement a static check that fires `fail` on any closed work-item   whose merge_evidence is null/missing despite the existence of an   unmerged feature branch in git topology.
- Add a migration script that backfills merge_evidence for already-  closed work-items by walking git log for the work-item id.
- Update capture-work-item / implement skills to populate merge_evidence   at closure time.

Per the parent PC body's §"Cross-cutting acceptance criteria": the epic is complete when (item 2) this child PC is revised + accepted in livespec-impl-plaintext. Until then, the LayerDifferent surfacer (Layer 3' doctor LLM phase, filed in livespec's queue) is the residual guardrail.
title: Implement work-item-merge-evidence schema fields per sibling child PC

description:
Cross-repo follow-up from livespec's /livespec:revise v081 (PC #2: coordinating-epic-stale-revise-enforcement, Sub-proposal 3 / Layer 2). Sibling repo work to honor the new contract.

Scope:
- Copy /data/projects/livespec/.github/workflows/auto-enable-merge.yml   byte-identically into livespec-impl-plaintext's .github/workflows/.
- Verify the App is installed on the repo (GitHub App with   enablePullRequestAutoMerge permission scope).
- Set repo secrets APP_ID + APP_PRIVATE_KEY via `gh secret set`.
- Test by opening any non-draft PR with author=thewoolleyman; verify   it auto-enables --auto --rebase.

Per the contract: drift between this file and livespec's canonical version will surface as a doctor-static finding via dev-tooling's check_branch_protection_alignment (or sibling check).
reason: superseded by li-mri7z6 — auto-enable-merge.yml propagated cross-repo via parent work-item; this child work-item is redundant
title: Add .github/workflows/auto-enable-merge.yml to livespec-impl-plaintext

description:
Follow-up impl work-item from livespec /livespec:revise v082 (livespec PC #3: revise-post-step-capture-impl-gaps, Layer 5 of the stale-revise-enforcement coordinating epic).

The livespec spec-side acceptance (v082) documents that the revise post-step invokes the active impl plugin's capture-impl-gaps skill with --since-version <prior-vN> to scope gap detection to the diff just introduced by the revise. The companion PC for this flag lives in this repo at SPECIFICATION/proposed_changes/capture-impl-gaps-since-version.md (filed 2026-05-26 — see commit 8d9d6b1 of this repo).

Scope:
- Implement --since-version <vNNN> flag on the capture-impl-gaps   thin-transport / heavyweight skill in   .claude-plugin/skills/capture-impl-gaps/SKILL.md and the   backing detect-impl-gaps thin-transport (per the spec-side   contract; capture-impl-gaps consults detect-impl-gaps).
- The flag scopes the gap-detection scan to the spec diff   between <prior-vN> and the latest cut version, instead of   re-detecting every long-standing gap.
- Without scoping, the livespec revise post-step would noisily   re-prompt for every legacy gap on every revise; with scoping,   only NEW gaps introduced by this revise surface — the   post-step's signal value stays high.
- Depends-on order: this work-item lands BEFORE livespec's   SKILL.md prose work-item (currently li-pzn5yp in livespec's   work-items.jsonl) can actually invoke the flag — the prose   references the flag prospectively; the IMPLEMENTATION needs   the flag to exist.
- Acceptance: the companion PC at   SPECIFICATION/proposed_changes/capture-impl-gaps-since-version.md   describes the full contract this work-item realizes.
title: Implement --since-version flag on capture-impl-gaps skill (Layer 5 of stale-revise enforcement)

description:
Follow-up impl work-item from livespec /livespec:revise v083 (livespec PC #4: spec-impl-commitment-tracking, sub-proposal 3 — extend-impl-plugin-contract-with-spec-commitment-hint).

The livespec spec-side acceptance (v083) declares that every impl-plugin's work-item record schema MUST carry an OPTIONAL spec_commitment_hint: string | null field, and that capture-work-item MUST accept an optional --spec-commitment-hint <id_hint> CLI flag (or backend-equivalent invocation surface).

Scope (in this repo):
- Add spec_commitment_hint: str | None field to WorkItem dataclass in .claude-plugin/scripts/livespec_impl_plaintext/types.py.
- Plumb the field through store.py's read/write paths (default null on read for legacy records; explicit serialization on write).
- Add --spec-commitment-hint <id_hint> flag to the capture-work-item skill surface (.claude-plugin/skills/capture-work-item/SKILL.md) and the backing Python entry point — when the flag is supplied, the resulting work-item's spec_commitment_hint MUST equal the verbatim id_hint; when omitted, the field defaults to null.
- Expose the field in list-work-items --json output (.claude-plugin/scripts/bin/list_work_items.py and its commands sibling) so livespec's unresolved-spec-commitment doctor invariant can match without invoking implementation-private machinery.
- Data migration: legacy work-items.jsonl records lacking the field MUST be readable with spec_commitment_hint defaulting to null (no in-place migration required; the field is OPTIONAL).
- Add --with-spec-commitment-hint <id_hint> filter flag to list-work-items for users who want to query by hint.
- Add unit + integration tests covering: write with flag, write without flag (null default), list-work-items --json output shape, filter flag behavior, legacy-record read compatibility.
- Reference: livespec SPECIFICATION/contracts.md §"Implementation-plugin contract — the 10-skill surface" → "Work-item `spec_commitment_hint` field" (landed in livespec v083 PC #4).

Depends-on order: this work-item lands BEFORE livespec's unresolved-spec-commitment doctor invariant module (currently li-7jniti in livespec's work-items.jsonl) can actually match against spec_commitment_hint records — the doctor invariant queries this skill's list-work-items --json output.
title: Add spec_commitment_hint field to work-item schema and --spec-commitment-hint flag to capture-work-item skill

description:
Cross-repo follow-up impl work-item from livespec /livespec:revise v085 (livespec PC #6: next-layer-3-discoverability-nudge).

The livespec spec change (landed in PR #262) adds a contract clause to SPECIFICATION/contracts.md §"Implementation-plugin contract — the 10-skill surface" → bullet `next` requiring every impl-plugin's `next` SKILL.md to surface a Layer 3 discoverability nudge on direct user invocation, parallel-and-symmetric to the /livespec:next requirement.

Scope:
- Edit .claude-plugin/skills/next/SKILL.md in this repo to add the discoverability nudge prose.
- The nudge MUST inform the user that .claude/skills/loop/SKILL.md (the project-local Layer 3 loop driver) is the cohesive cross-side composition surface combining /livespec:next with the active impl-plugin's next (i.e., this repo's `next` skill), and MUST ask the user to confirm direct invocation rather than via /loop.
- The nudge MUST skip when this skill is invoked by another skill (Layer 3 driver itself, doctor cross-boundary surface, etc.) rather than by a direct user request. Detection mechanism is per-harness.
- The impl-plugin author MAY specialize the wording to reference the JSONL substrate (this repo's backend) but MUST preserve the load-bearing semantics: inform the user that the Layer 3 driver is the cohesive cross-side surface, and ask them to confirm direct invocation.
- The wrapper at .claude-plugin/scripts/bin/next.py MUST remain a pure thin-transport pass-through; the nudge is SKILL.md-prose discipline only.
- For projects where .claude/skills/loop/SKILL.md is absent, the nudge MAY soften to a documentation pointer.
- Reference: livespec SPECIFICATION/contracts.md §"Implementation-plugin contract — the 10-skill surface" → bullet `next` (final clause), and §"`/livespec:next` spec-side thin-transport skill" → §"Layer 3 discoverability nudge" for the load-bearing semantics it references.

Paired same-repo follow-up in livespec is li-x6zfw8 (livespec PR #263) for that repo's own /livespec:next SKILL.md.
title: Add Layer 3 discoverability nudge prose to skills/next/SKILL.md (per livespec PC #6)
Update next.py wrapper to emit {candidates[], pagination} envelope per spec v005
description:
Spec commitment hint: next-output-schema-contradicts-upstream (PC #2 sub-proposal A in SPECIFICATION/history/v005/proposed_changes/claude-opus-4-7-critique.md).

v005 of SPECIFICATION/contracts.md §next rewrote the documented output schema from the flat {action, work_item_ref, urgency, reason} single-object shape to the upstream {candidates[], pagination} envelope, with new --limit (default 5) and --offset (default 0) flags that MUST exit 2 on invalid (non-positive --limit, negative --offset).

The wrapper at .claude-plugin/scripts/bin/next.py and the underlying ranker in livespec_impl_plaintext/commands/next.py still emit the legacy flat shape and do not accept --limit / --offset. This work-item closes that gap via TDD: Red (failing tests covering the new envelope shape + --limit/--offset validation + empty candidates[] no-work signal) then Green (ranker enumerates all ripe candidates, applies offset+limit, wraps in pagination envelope).

Acceptance: wrapper emits {candidates[], pagination} per v005 contract; --limit positive-int validation exits 2 on invalid; --offset non-negative-int validation exits 2 on invalid; empty candidates[] IS the no-work signal; impl-plaintext-specific fields (priority, origin) MAY ride on candidates without additionalProperties discipline. Full just check passes.

The wrapper at .claude-plugin/scripts/bin/next.py and the underlying ranker in livespec_impl_plaintext/commands/next.py still emit the legacy flat shape and do not accept --limit / --offset. This work-item closes that gap via TDD: Red (failing tests covering the new envelope shape + --limit/--offset validation + empty candidates[] no-work signal) then Green (ranker enumerates all ripe candidates, applies offset+limit, wraps in pagination envelope).

Acceptance: wrapper emits {candidates[], pagination} per v005 contract; --limit positive-int validation exits 2 on invalid; --offset non-negative-int validation exits 2 on invalid; empty candidates[] IS the no-work signal; impl-plaintext-specific fields (priority, origin) MAY ride on candidates without additionalProperties discipline. Full just check passes.
title: Update next.py wrapper to emit {candidates[], pagination} envelope per spec v005

description:
Surfaced by check-claude-md-coverage once wired into the canonical aggregate (li-ipxwir, epic li-univck Phase 3.1).

## Background

The shared check claude_md_coverage requires every directory under `.claude-plugin/scripts/`, `tests/`, and `dev-tooling/` (modulo `_vendor/`, `fixtures/`, `__pycache__/`) to carry a CLAUDE.md describing the local constraints an agent working in that directory must satisfy. impl-plaintext currently lacks CLAUDE.md files in 11 in-scope directories:

- .claude-plugin/scripts
- .claude-plugin/scripts/bin
- .claude-plugin/scripts/livespec_impl_plaintext
- .claude-plugin/scripts/livespec_impl_plaintext/commands
- .claude-plugin/scripts/livespec_impl_plaintext/migration
- tests
- tests/bin
- tests/livespec_impl_plaintext
- tests/livespec_impl_plaintext/commands
- tests/livespec_impl_plaintext/migration
- dev-tooling

## Scope

Author a minimal CLAUDE.md in each of the 11 directories listed above. Each file should describe the directory's role, the discipline an agent must follow when editing files there, and pointers to load-bearing constraints (per-file-ignores, test fixtures, vendoring rules, etc.).

## Acceptance

- `just check-claude-md-coverage` exits 0.
- Each CLAUDE.md is non-empty, names the directory's purpose, and references the canonical authority (livespec/SPECIFICATION/ or impl-plaintext's local SPECIFICATION/) where applicable.
title: Author CLAUDE.md in 11 in-scope directories for claude-md-coverage compliance

description:
Surfaced by check-heading-coverage once wired into the canonical aggregate (li-ipxwir, epic li-univck Phase 3.1).

## Background

The shared check heading_coverage requires every `## ` heading in the spec tree (SPECIFICATION/spec.md, contracts.md, constraints.md, scenarios.md) to have a corresponding entry in tests/heading-coverage.json. impl-plaintext has 27 spec headings missing from the coverage map. Per livespec's revise co-edit discipline, the coverage map is normally maintained in lockstep with spec revisions; this is a one-time backfill for the existing baseline.

## Scope

1. Enumerate every `## ` heading in SPECIFICATION/*.md (spec.md, contracts.md, constraints.md, scenarios.md).
2. Author an entry per heading in tests/heading-coverage.json with `TODO` + `reason` placeholders (the v064 pattern documented in livespec/.claude/CLAUDE.md), OR map each heading to its concrete coverage test where one exists.
3. Verify `just check-heading-coverage` exits 0.

## Acceptance

- `just check-heading-coverage` exits 0.
- tests/heading-coverage.json structurally mirrors livespec's heading-coverage.json (per livespec/SPECIFICATION/non-functional-requirements.md heading-coverage rules).
title: Backfill tests/heading-coverage.json for 27 baseline spec headings

description:
Surfaced by check-wrapper-shape once wired into the canonical aggregate (li-ipxwir, epic li-univck Phase 3.1).

## Background

The shared check wrapper_shape (livespec_dev_tooling.checks.wrapper_shape) hardcodes a livespec-prefix coupling in `_is_livespec_main_import`:

    stmt.module.startswith("livespec.")

This rejects livespec-impl-plaintext's wrappers, which correctly import `from livespec_impl_plaintext.commands.<name> import main` (the impl-plugin package name, NOT the livespec spec-side package name). The wrappers at .claude-plugin/scripts/bin/{detect_impl_gaps,list_memos,list_work_items,migrate_beads,next}.py all conform to the canonical 5-statement shebang shape but fail the prefix gate.

## Fix location

**Upstream in livespec-dev-tooling**, not in this repo. The check needs to either:

1. Accept any non-empty module name (drop the prefix gate; the structural 5-statement shape is the load-bearing invariant), OR
2. Read the accepted prefix from a per-repo config knob (e.g., [tool.livespec_dev_tooling] role-key), OR
3. Auto-detect the project package name from `pyproject.toml` `[project] name`.

Approach 1 is the simplest and matches the docstring's intent (the load-bearing invariant is 'has main import + raises SystemExit(main())'; the prefix is incidental).

## Scope (livespec-dev-tooling)

1. File a PR against livespec-dev-tooling relaxing the prefix gate.
2. Bump-pin impl-plaintext to the resulting v0.4.x release (mechanical bump).
3. Verify `just check-wrapper-shape` exits 0 against impl-plaintext.

## Acceptance

- `just check-wrapper-shape` exits 0 from impl-plaintext primary checkout.
- livespec and livespec-dev-tooling self-host the same relaxed check (no regression).
title: Relax wrapper_shape livespec-prefix coupling (upstream livespec-dev-tooling fix)

description:
## Repro

1. Install `livespec-impl-plaintext` via the Claude Code marketplace; cache resolves to `~/.claude/plugins/cache/livespec-impl-plaintext/livespec-impl-plaintext/<sha>/` (observed against `de4f836a7d4f` and the current master HEAD `255d0a4`).
2. From any project root, invoke `/livespec-impl-plaintext:next` (or `list-work-items`, or anything that imports `livespec_runtime`).
3. The wrapper exits non-zero with:

    ```
    File ".../scripts/livespec_impl_plaintext/commands/next.py", line 58, in <module>
        from livespec_runtime.cross_repo.types import CrossRepoManifest
    ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'livespec_runtime'
    ```

## Root cause

The plugin's own constraint (`SPECIFICATION/constraints.md` §"Vendored dependencies in `.claude-plugin/scripts/_vendor/`; no PyPI runtime dependencies.") mandates a populated `_vendor/` tree. `git log --all --diff-filter=A -- '*_vendor*'` returns empty — `_vendor/` has never been committed at any SHA. The bootstrap at `.claude-plugin/scripts/bin/_bootstrap.py:16-21` adds `scripts/_vendor` to `sys.path`, but the directory does not exist in the published cache.

Dev-mode `uv sync` papers over this in the primary checkout (where `pyproject.toml` + `uv.lock` populate `.venv/`), but the marketplace install copies **neither** `pyproject.toml` nor `uv.lock` into the cache — so there is no path by which `livespec_runtime` is importable for an end-user invocation.

Smoking gun: commit `1cbbb7a` ("chore(next): wire resolve_ref into impl-plaintext next ranker") said in its message "No new third-party deps; the impl-plaintext bundle stays empty-vendor." and then introduced these runtime imports:

- `.claude-plugin/scripts/livespec_impl_plaintext/commands/next.py:58` — `from livespec_runtime.cross_repo.types import CrossRepoManifest`
- `.claude-plugin/scripts/livespec_impl_plaintext/commands/_cross_repo.py:29-31` — three imports from `livespec_runtime.cross_repo.{errors,resolve,types}`
- `.claude-plugin/scripts/livespec_impl_plaintext/commands/list_work_items.py:40` — `from livespec_runtime.cross_repo.types import CrossRepoManifest`

The intent and the code disagree.

## Reference: sibling plugin does it right

The `livespec` plugin (same family, same template) ships a populated `_vendor/livespec_runtime/` tree in-repo, including a LICENSE file naming the vendored copy. Same pattern, just not applied here.

## Fix sketch

1. Vendor `livespec_runtime` into `.claude-plugin/scripts/_vendor/livespec_runtime/` mirroring the layout in the `livespec` sibling.
2. Wire a `just vendor-sync` recipe (referenced in justfile comments at lines 23, 159, but not implemented) that pulls from `github.com/thewoolleyman/livespec-runtime` at the version pinned in `[tool.uv.sources]` (currently `v0.3.0` after #49) and re-vendors on each pin bump.
3. Add a vendor-manifest check (also referenced in justfile comments) that fails CI if the vendored copy drifts from the pin.
4. Consider whether `pyproject.toml` belongs in the marketplace bundle too, as a belt-and-suspenders fallback — but vendoring is the spec-mandated path.

## Acceptance

- `~/.claude/plugins/cache/livespec-impl-plaintext/livespec-impl-plaintext/<sha>/scripts/_vendor/livespec_runtime/` exists in a fresh install.
- `/livespec-impl-plaintext:next` and `/livespec-impl-plaintext:list-work-items` succeed against a stock cache install with no `uv sync` step.
- `git log --all --diff-filter=A -- '*_vendor*'` is non-empty.
title: Plugin bundle ships without _vendor/livespec_runtime/ — every cross-repo skill fails with ModuleNotFoundError from the installed cache

description:
## Repro

1. Install the plugin via marketplace; cache lands at `~/.claude/plugins/cache/livespec-impl-plaintext/livespec-impl-plaintext/<sha>/`.
2. Inspect the cache layout:

    ```
    cache/.../<sha>/scripts/bin/next.py          ← actual wrapper location
    cache/.../<sha>/.claude-plugin/              ← does NOT exist; flattened away
    ```

3. Read any SKILL.md (e.g. `skills/next/SKILL.md`):

    ```bash
    uv run python3 .claude-plugin/scripts/bin/next.py "$@"
    ```

4. Running that command literally — which is what a Claude Code agent does when it follows the skill instructions — yields:

    ```
    python3: can't open file '.../<sha>/.claude-plugin/scripts/bin/next.py':
    [Errno 2] No such file or directory
    ```

## Root cause

Claude Code's plugin installer flattens `.claude-plugin/scripts/` → `scripts/` (and `.claude-plugin/skills/` → `skills/`) when materializing the cache. The marketplace source tree's `.claude-plugin/` prefix is a publication convention; it is **not preserved** in the runtime cache. Every SKILL.md in this plugin documents paths in the source-repo form, which doesn't survive the install.

Affected files (every SKILL.md that quotes a wrapper path):
- `skills/next/SKILL.md`
- `skills/list-work-items/SKILL.md`
- `skills/list-memos/SKILL.md`
- `skills/detect-impl-gaps/SKILL.md`
- `skills/capture-work-item/SKILL.md`
- `skills/capture-memo/SKILL.md`
- `skills/capture-impl-gaps/SKILL.md`
- `skills/capture-spec-drift/SKILL.md`
- `skills/implement/SKILL.md`
- `skills/process-memos/SKILL.md`

## Secondary contributor

The cache also omits `pyproject.toml`, `uv.lock`, and `.python-version`, so `uv run` from the cache cannot synthesize a venv as a fallback either. Combined with Bug 1 (missing `_vendor/`), an end-user invocation has no working path.

## Fix sketch

Two options:

1. **Document the flattened layout.** Rewrite the `## Invocation` block in every SKILL.md to quote `scripts/bin/<name>.py` (or whatever the post-flatten cache path is), with a parenthetical note explaining the convention. This is the smallest change but couples the docs to Claude Code's installer behavior.
2. **Use a stable redirector.** Ship a single wrapper at a path that survives flattening (e.g. `.claude-plugin/scripts/bin/<name>.py` → kept as a `bin/<name>.py` shim that dispatches to whatever layout exists). Either way the SKILL.md cites the stable name and works in both source-repo and cache contexts.

Option 2 is more robust; option 1 is faster. Pick whichever the maintainer prefers, but every SKILL.md needs to change.

## Acceptance

- An agent reading any SKILL.md and running its quoted invocation verbatim succeeds against a fresh cache install (combined with Bug 1's fix, so `livespec_runtime` is importable).
- A CI check verifies SKILL.md `## Invocation` blocks reference paths that exist post-flatten.

## Related

Sibling repos using the same template (`livespec`, `livespec-runtime`, `livespec-dev-tooling`) likely share this issue — worth checking after the fix lands here.
title: SKILL.md invocation paths assume source-repo layout — Claude Code's `.claude-plugin/`-flattening installer breaks every wrapper path agents see

description: Close-out: authored CLAUDE.md in the 11 in-scope directories missing one, satisfying the shared claude_md_coverage check.
reason: Closed. Each describes the directory's actual contents and the local constraints an agent editing it must satisfy (keyword-only args, append-only JSONL discipline, supervisor-only stdout/stderr, query-only contract, one-shot non-idempotent migrations, wrapper no-logic shape, --no-auto-install hook discipline, etc.), modeled on the livespec sibling's per-directory CLAUDE.md files and adapted to the livespec_impl_plaintext package. `mise exec -- just check-claude-md-coverage` exits 0. Doc-only changeset (zero .py files).

Created tests/heading-coverage.json (did not previously exist) with one entry per SPECIFICATION/{spec,contracts,constraints,scenarios}.md H2 heading flagged by the heading_coverage check (27 entries total). Each entry carries the (spec_root, spec_file, heading) triple plus test="TODO" and a non-empty reason explaining the baseline backfill, mirroring the livespec sibling's v064 TODO+reason pattern; no fabricated test references. `mise exec -- just check-heading-coverage` exits 0. Doc/config-only changeset (zero .py files).
title: Backfill tests/heading-coverage.json for 27 baseline spec headings

description: Close-out: relaxed wrapper_shape upstream in livespec-dev-tooling v0.5.1 and bumped this repo's pin from v0.5.0 to v0.5.1.
reason: Closed. The wrapper_shape prefix coupling was relaxed upstream in livespec-dev-tooling v0.5.1 to accept livespec_impl_plaintext.* wrapper imports (the load-bearing invariant is the 5-statement shebang shape + `raise SystemExit(main())`, not the package-name prefix). Bumped [tool.uv.sources] livespec-dev-tooling pin from tag v0.5.0 to v0.5.1; re-ran uv lock + uv sync. `mise exec -- just check-wrapper-shape` now exits 0, and the full `mise exec -- just check` aggregate is GREEN (all 40 targets pass). With check-claude-md-coverage and check-heading-coverage already green (li-bb5suo, li-4liaxt), all three previously-failing gate checks now pass. Doc/config-only changeset (zero .py files: pyproject.toml + uv.lock + work-items.jsonl).
title: Relax wrapper_shape livespec-prefix coupling (upstream livespec-dev-tooling fix)

description: Close-out: vendored livespec_runtime v0.3.0 into .claude-plugin/scripts/_vendor/livespec_runtime/ (10 files, byte-identical to the livespec sibling) and added a single-entry .vendor.jsonc manifest so installed-cache skills import livespec_runtime.cross_repo.* without uv sync.
reason: Closed. Root cause: the published marketplace bundle copied neither pyproject.toml nor uv.lock and never committed a _vendor/ tree, so the runtime imports introduced in commit 1cbbb7a (livespec_runtime.cross_repo.{errors,resolve,types}) died with ModuleNotFoundError on a stock cache install. Fix: copied the 10-file livespec_runtime v0.3.0 tree (LICENSE, __init__.py, py.typed, cross_repo/{__init__,errors,resolve,retry,types}.py, cross_repo/providers/{__init__,github}.py) verbatim from the CI-validated livespec sibling at the matching pin into .claude-plugin/scripts/_vendor/livespec_runtime/ (verified byte-identical via cmp, no __pycache__), and authored .vendor.jsonc with a single livespec_runtime entry (upstream_url https://github.com/thewoolleyman/livespec-runtime, upstream_ref v0.3.0, ISO vendored_at, no shim flag) that check-vendor-manifest validates. The bootstrap already adds scripts/_vendor/ to sys.path, so no bootstrap change was needed; impl-plaintext imports only livespec_runtime.cross_repo.*, so no further vendoring was required. Commit prefix chore(deps): (the red-green-replay commit-msg hook classifies _vendor/ + .vendor.jsonc as exempt non-product paths and rejects fix:), mirroring the sibling precedent 258ba89. Import sanity-check (sys.path-insert + from livespec_runtime.cross_repo.types import CrossRepoManifest) passes with no uv sync; git log --all --diff-filter=A -- '*_vendor*' is now non-empty; full mise exec -- just check is GREEN including check-vendor-manifest; no .py test added (_vendor/ is third-party, exempt from tests-mirror-pairing; existing test_cross_repo.py already exercises the package).
title: Plugin bundle ships without _vendor/livespec_runtime/ — every cross-repo skill fails with ModuleNotFoundError from the installed cache
